title: Week of July 6, 2026: County Road Money, School Sports Fees, and Oakdale City Hall
summary:
This week: the Washington County Board weighs nearly $4 million in road-project spending (July 7); the Stillwater school district raises middle-school sports fees to about $200 per sport; Oakdale's City Hall and police lobby relocate for an 18-month, voter-tax-funded rebuild; and the Stillwater City Council's July 7 consent agenda includes several contract awards and a school resource officer agreement.

HOW TO GET INVOLVED
- Washington County Board: Tue, July 7, 9:00 AM, County Board Room, Government Center, Stillwater. Public comment period; fill out a comment card before the meeting.
- Stillwater City Council: Tue, July 7, workshop 4:30 PM / regular 7:00 PM, Stillwater City Hall.
- Oakdale Planning Commission: Thu, July 9; two public hearings scheduled.
- County public-comment windows (online at washingtoncountymn.gov): proposed regional trail (Middle St. Croix Valley to Phalen-Keller) closes July 10; County Road 10/17 intersection near Lake Elmo closes July 12.
- Stillwater middle-school sports fee: the final amount is set before mid-July registration; watch the district's Community Education page.
- Stuff the Bus school-supply help: register through July 23 at uwwce.org/stuff-the-bus.
- Also coming up: County Board and Oakdale City Council on July 14; Stillwater City Council July 21; Stillwater school board July 28.

Hosted by Artie Fishel. Sources: official city, county, and school-district agendas and minutes.
